LAW AND MOTION 301, RE: DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCE WAS MADE BY ANY PARTY, COUNSEL, OR INTERESTED PERSON. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT ACTALENT SCIENTIFIC LLC'S DEMURRER TO ALL SEVEN CAUSES OF ACTION ALLEGED AGAINST IT IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF KIMBERLY MAYS IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-13-2026 Continued To Sep-09-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-16-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT ACTALENT SCIENTIFIC LLC'S DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Actalent Scientific LLC's demurrer to all seven causes of action alleged against it in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Kimberly Mays is sustained with leave to amend as to the second cause of action for hostile work environment, the third cause of action for failure to prevent, and the eighth cause of action for constructive discharge and sustained without leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action for negligent hiring, retention and/or supervision, the fifth cause of action for sexual assault and battery, the sixth cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress, and the seventh cause of action for negligent infliction of emotional distress.

Mays fails to allege the required elements of any of her claims against Actalent with the required ultimate facts.

Mays' request for leave to amend is granted as to the harassment and constructive discharge claims because there is a reasonable possibility that she might be able to allege sufficient ultimate facts to support those claims. Mays is also granted leave to amend to allege a general negligence claim, not a negligent hiring/retention/supervision claim, based on Actalent's alleged conduct toward Mays. However, Mays is admonished that conclusory statements (i.e. those that parrot required elements of a claim) will not suffice. If Mays wishes to file a second amended complaint, she must do so by May 5, 2026.

Mays' request for leave to amend is denied as to the negligent hiring/retention/supervision, sexual violence and emotional distress claims because she has not proffered any basis to believe that she might be able to allege sufficient ultimate facts to support these claims. The negligent hiring/retention/supervision and sexual violence claims are only cognizable if Actalent employed or contracted with Fifer and it is apparent that Mays has no information that Actalent did so. The intentional infliction claim requires a level of intentionality that Mays does not suggest occurred. As Mays acknowledges, negligent infliction of emotional distress is not a cognizable claim.

A proposed order has been submitted.

Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/HEK)
